title: coussarea cornifolia
intro: Coussarea cornifolia, also known as the "Yellow Root" or "Yellow Dogwood", is a medicinal plant native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Coussarea cornifolia include relief from fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, as well as being used in traditional medicine to make tinctures and ointments.
